statement: Invariant mcp-scan (the engine inside Snyk Agent Scan) statically scans MCP/agent artefacts for prompt injection and tool poisoning before use and pins tool hashes to detect post-approval tampering (rug pulls).

evidence.quote: MCP-Scan checks installed MCP servers for prompt injections, tool poisoning... Built-in Tool Pinning detects MCP Rug Pull attacks, verifying integrity of installed tools by tracking changes via tool hashing.
Discover and scan agent components on your machine for prompt injections and vulnerabilities (including agents, MCP servers, skills).

falsifier: If tool-pinning/hashing were removed or shown to be runtime-only, the integrity-checking overlap would be weaker.
